title: Artifact import utility with type detection and API integration
purpose: Provides functionality to import files as various artifact types by detecting their extension and calling appropriate API endpoints.
module_context: Exports type definitions for ArtifactType and ForcedType, and constants for image extensions. Includes helper functions for filename processing and the main importArtifact function.

steps.content: Check if the filename ends with .design.json and return design type.
Check if the filename ends with .spreadsheet.json and return spreadsheet type.
Check if the filename ends with .mmd and return diagram type.
Check if the filename ends with .md and return document type.
Extract the file extension and check if it's an image extension.
IF the extension is an image extension, return image type.
ELSE return snippet type with the full filename.
Check if the filename ends with .design.json and remove the extension.
Check if the filename ends with .spreadsheet.json and remove the extension.
Check if the filename ends with .mmd and remove the extension.
Check if the filename ends with .md and remove the extension.
IF none of the known extensions match, return the filename unchanged.
Split the filename by dots and take the last part.
Convert the extension to lowercase.
IF there is no extension, return an empty string.

methods.name: importArtifact
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, file: File, opts?: { forcedType?: ForcedType }): Promise<ImportResult>

steps.content: IF a forced type is specified, process the file according to that type.
IF the forced type is image, validate the extension and upload the file to the image endpoint.
IF the forced type is design, validate the extension and upload the file to the design endpoint.
IF the forced type is spreadsheet, validate the extension and upload the file to the spreadsheet endpoint.
IF the forced type is diagram, document, or snippet, upload the file to the corresponding endpoint.
IF the forced type is code-file, attempt to create a linked snippet and fall back to regular snippet if that fails.
ELSE detect the artifact type from the filename and process accordingly.
IF the detected type is image, upload the file to the image endpoint.
ELSE read the file content and parse JSON for design and spreadsheet types.
Upload the file content to the appropriate API endpoint based on the detected type.
Return the created artifact's type and server-assigned id.
